channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Liberation Hero’s Son Arrested After Gun And Assault Allegations At School Rugby Match
item.description: Eaglesvale-CBC Rugby Match Turns Chaotic As Parent Faces Gun Charges A Harare parent who is the son of late national hero Alfred Nikita Mangena has been arrested and charged following a violent incident that unfolded after a schools rugby match between Eaglesvale College and Christian Brothers College (CBC) on Saturday, 13 June 2026. Lotshe Yuri [...] The post Liberation Hero’s Son Arrested After Gun And Assault Allegations At School Rugby Match appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Musarara Withdraws Legal Bid to Stop Grain Import Levy Framework @263Chat @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: The Grain Millers Association of Zimbabwe (GMAZ), chaired by suspended ZANU-PF Mazowe District Coordinating Committee (DCC) chairman Tafadzwa Musarara, has withdrawn its High Court application challenging Government’s grain import levy framework introduced under Statutory Instrument 87 of 2025. According to a Notice of Withdrawal filed [...] The post Musarara Withdraws Legal Bid to Stop Grain Import Levy Framework appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: FIU moves to regulate Virtual Asset Service @HeraldZimbabwe
item.description: Nqobile Bhebhe, ZIMBABWE has taken a major step towards regulating the rapidly growing virtual assets sector by introducing a framework for the registration and supervision of Virtual Asset Service Providers... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Herald

item.title: Registering With the FIU Doesn’t Make Your Crypto Business Legal, FIU Says @Techzim
item.description: After S.I. 99 of 2026 landed last week, plenty of people (me included) read it as Zimbabwe finally giving crypto some kind of green light. The Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU) has now put out a public notice that says, in effect, not so fast. As promised we sent the FIU some que #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Techzim

item.title: Ramaphosa urges South Africans to stop blaming migrants for local issues @CyrilRamaphosa
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa on Tuesday urged South Africans not to blame migrants for the country’s problems, after a recent wave of anti-immigrant unrest and violence. One of Africa’s largest economies, South Africa has long attracted migrant workers from across the continent, both legally and illegally. But in recent weeks, mobs of South Africans carrying sticks, [...] The post Ramaphosa urges South Africans to stop blaming migrants for local issues appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: MPs sit past midnight as opposition resists constitutional bill stampede
item.description: CCC MP thrown out during angry exchanges The post MPs sit past midnight as opposition resists constitutional bill stampede appeared first on Zimbabwe News Now. (via Zimbabwean News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwean News

item.title: AI cameras from these 20 different spots in Harare catch 15,000 traffic offending drivers: Here is how you will be notified & how to pay fines
item.description: The Silent Sentinels: How AI Cameras Are Rewriting the Rules of Harare’s Roads HARARE – The era of the “cat and mouse” game between motorists and traffic police on the streets of Harare is coming to a swift, digital end. In a massive technological shift that has caught thousands of drivers off guard, a new [...] The post AI cameras from these 20 different spots in Harare catch 15,000 traffic offending drivers: Here is how you will be notified & how to pay fines first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Legal Discussions with Vengai Madzima: Investing in Business and Knowledge Process Outsourcing
item.description: NewZimbabwe.com has invited Mr. Vengai Madzima, the Senior Partner at Madzima Chidyausiku Museta Legal Practitioners (MCM Legal), to discuss with us legal issues that affect Zimbabweans. The discussions are of a general nature and those seeking specific legal advice should contact their lawyer. Reporter: Welcome back Mr. Madzima. This week, we want to discuss investing [...] The post Legal Discussions with Vengai Madzima: Investing in Business and Knowledge Process Outsourcing appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Resident sues City of Harare over Helcraw prepaid water meters
item.description: A Harare resident has filed an urgent High Court application seeking to stop the rollout of prepaid water meters in the capital, arguing that the system is unlawful, violates existing water laws and effectively denies residents access to a basic human right. Bernadette Makaya, a ratepayer from Mabelreign represented by Zimbabwe Lawyers for Human Rights’ [...] The post Resident sues City of Harare over Helcraw prepaid water meters appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe repatriates 660 from South Africa as xenophobia tensions escalate
item.description: ZIMBABWE has begun repatriating its nationals from South Africa amid growing fears of possible xenophobic attacks ahead of a June 30 deadline set by an anti-immigration pressure group, with 660 citizens having returned home so far. The development comes as tensions rise across South Africa following threats by self-styled anti-immigrant group March and March, which [...] The post Zimbabwe repatriates 660 from South Africa as xenophobia tensions escalate appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: SADC Ministers to meet in Zimbabwe to drive regional infrastructure, AI integration
item.description: Channel Africa The Southern African Development Community (SADC) Secretariat will convene a high-level ministerial cluster meeting in Bulawayo next week to co-ordinate policies on regional transport, communications and climate resilience. Scheduled to run from June 22 to 26, 2026, the summit brings together Ministers and senior policymakers from all member states. The sessions will be [...] The post SADC Ministers to meet in Zimbabwe to drive regional infrastructure, AI integration appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Chitungwiza Municipality orders removal of pool tables at unauthorised sites within seven days over drug abuse fears
item.description: CHITUNGWIZA Municipality has ordered the removal of pool tables operating at unauthorised sites within seven working days, saying that some of the facilities have become hubs for drug dealing. These unauthorised sites include residential areas, overnight parking bays, tuckshops, shebeens, road servitudes and public transport terminals. The directive was adopted during the municipality’s 508th Ordinary [...] The post Chitungwiza Municipality orders removal of pool tables at unauthorised sites within seven days over drug abuse fears appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Controversial Zimbabwean tycoon Wicknell Chivayo wins a stake in Kenya’s $2.9 billion airport expansion deal @wicknellchivayo
item.description: Billionaires Africa Controversial Zimbabwean businessman Wicknell Chivayo, who has close ties to Kenyan President William Ruto, has secured a stake in the Sh375 billion ($2.9 billion) tender to expand Jomo Kenyatta International Airport, reigniting concerns about the country’s government procurement transparency less than two years after the same project’s previous award to India’s Adani Group [...] The post Controversial Zimbabwean tycoon Wicknell Chivayo wins a stake in Kenya’s $2.9 billion airport expansion deal appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: How does a whole cabinet agree that domestic workers deserve a measly $90? @bulawayo24
item.description: ​It seems that in Zimbabwe, some workers are simply deemed unworthy.There is a profound, stomach-turning irony in policy decisions that pretend to be gifts while delivering nothing but insult. If... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: NetOne’s OneMoney powers financial inclusion, creates jobs and eases transfers through dense distribution network @bulawayo24 @NetOneCellular
item.description: Here is your clean, corrected and Bulawayo24‐ready rewrite, in British English, sentence case, straight quotes, legally safe, and aligned with your business‐and‐technology development desk style.No bu... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Pro‐CAB3 rally cancelled as opposition claims backing for Bill is fading, venue ’haunted’ @bulawayo24 @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Harare – A planned “citizens’ solidarity” rally in support of Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3), organised by Zanu PF, was cancelled this week, triggering competing claims about the Bill’s pu... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: 9 killed, 25 injured as bus slams into train in Triangle @zimsituation
item.description: 2 children among dead after bus driver fails to stop for train Source: 9 killed, 25 injured as bus slams into train in Triangle – Zimbabwe News Now Damage to bus after train collision in Triangle on June 16, 2026 MASVINGO — Nine people, including two children, were killed and at least 25 others hospitalised on [...] The post 9 killed, 25 injured as bus slams into train in Triangle appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
